title: cerastium rectum subsp. petricola
intro: Cerastium rectum subsp. petricola, also known as a subspecies of the common chickweed, is a flowering plant native to certain regions in Europe, North America, and Asia, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and hairy stems. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cerastium rectum subsp. petricola include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and easing symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Cerastium rectum subsp. petricola can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical salve for its medicinal and health benefits.
